title: Allow admins to administer ALL boards

posts.author: Stefan (SJN)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday,

I strongly suggest to give admin users the ability to see and do everything with every board (except private boards of course).

At this moment, an administrator cannot do his work, since he is not aware of the existence of shareable boards, unless someone subscribed him to them.

Once subscribed, he may be an admin, but still cannot change board subscribers and change everything he deems necessary, unless he is assigned ‘board owner’ by the registered board owners (who may be users that have left).

With the current permissions, he needs to be subscribed to EVERY board to be able to administer it, meaning he receives tons of updates and notifications about things he’s absolutely not interested in. Updates and notifications where he is interested in, easily get lost in the bulk of ‘trash’.

And all of that, only because an admin doesn’t have true admin rights at the moment

posts.author: sheldoninboxdesign (Sheldon)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree 100% - We’ve had a fair bit of confusion about tasks not getting done because the right people can’t see boards.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here.

I’m surprised that admins cannot see shared boards created by others. As mentioned above, this causes problems when those people move on and tasks remain incomplete.

posts.author: AlliS (Alli Spoor)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes I agree re admins having admin rights to ALL boards. I asked another staff member to set up a board for me as I didn’t have time myself and now any adjustments to the board or additional users etc have to go through her log in instead.

posts.author: Stefan (SJN)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for the support to this request, @AlliS.
In your particular case, your staff member can simply make you board owner (see board subscribers) on which you get the same rights as her on that particular board

posts.author: crparrett (Christian Parrett)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? We have multiple Admins and hundreds of boards so going through each to make this change would be very in-efficient.

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is actually a GDPR concern for us. We need to administer our data, and we don’t want to make the users who didn’t share boards with us (as admins) feel awkward when we “take ownership”, so we have lots of unknown monday boards flying around with people not well versed in how to handle certain compliance concerns, hiding them from us.

What’s up with this one Monday?

posts.author: vancestone (Vance)
posts.cookedHtml: Also agree. Not sure why @Monday your not listening to this feedback!!

posts.author: abiciea (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: Poor administrative control is a big reason we are not implementing Monday across our entire business.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
abiciea:

Poor administrative control

Yes, that is a weakness we are noticing with Monday.

It needs more comprehensive admin controls and more granular permissions for users.

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d like to join the group on this topic.

Because we operate ONLY on Private Boards Sctrictly, this entire stats section is useless to us. Bummer … BUT! if Monday.com could enhance the administrator access/tools, it would actually be helpful.

image1298×510 24.6 KB

posts.author: rachel.findlay (Rachel Findlay)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a massive issue we have also. It would even be better if admin could atleast see all of the boards that exist and who is owner/is subscribed to them (not necessarily the information thats on them).

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
That could work, but should be configurable by Monday customer. For instance - we need someone to see what data we store - that’s a must, but I could see where some organizations would not want to give the IT level admin resources that ability.

Perhaps added security groups/ of some sort could help Monday here - different rights for different types of admin access.

posts.author: clcpm (Chris M)
posts.cookedHtml: I am literally shocked that this is even an issue. But we report bugs to Monday.com without hearing back on a weekly basis so I guess I should not be so shocked.

posts.author: sheldoninboxdesign (Sheldon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday Team - is this in your pipeline?
I’ve been a requested feature (and an obvious feature) for a while now and no response to your community.

posts.author: jdd (J Davison)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this not a data protection problem? Or is there a different tool for dealing with subject access requests? If my org receives a subject access request, we are legally required to inform the requester about all the information we are storing about them. How are we supposed to do that if admins can’t search all boards for the personal information? We can’t rely on employees thoroughly searching the boards they own and reporting back to us, we need an admin account to be able to search for it.

posts.author: GUM (Ryan at GUM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Echoing the concerns above, our company as a whole struggles to use monday to its fullest potential because each user struggles to configure their notifications, dashboards, boards, etc, in ways that would be most useful to them and others.

If an admin user with actual admin access could alter the settings for each user and modify permissions and board structures across the company, we could more quickly and efficiently develop our internal procedures for using monday effectively.

This is a major oversight in monday’s design and application for businesses. Until this feature is available, our company can only commit to a monthly subscription, as we have to stay on the lookout for another platform that can be better curated and customized without relying on each individual user for the heavy lifting of setup and upkeep.

posts.author: morhriveion (Robert Hollon)
posts.cookedHtml: Really hoping the Monday team addresses this. The comment above about “how to” concerning passing ownership fails to recognize the needs of multiple industries. At a high level, we are building out Monday as the front end for data management just as others have mentioned. For any system that houses data, there needs to be a user class that can access the ENTIRE infrastructure, with partitioned permissions below that class. I understand that is against the concept of an open networking model, but it is absolutely critical from both system and compliance levels to have access to existing boards and data.

posts.author: Jody (Jody Poland)
posts.cookedHtml: This is my second attempt with a Monday account. They were much more supportive and responsive when they were DaPulse before receiving venture capital. We too really need permissions and admin oversight.

posts.author: SteveE (Stephen Emery)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thought I’d bring this request back from the dead - not having complete sight of all boards as administrator devalues the purpose of an administrator.

Any update?

posts.author: Yuth (Yuth)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree that this would be a great feature to have to keep all the board in places and control the board creation to manageable level…

posts.author: jwhip (Jessica Whipple)
posts.cookedHtml:
Posting so that Monday knows people are still invested in this topic… With all of the capability and customization available in this program, I can’t believe the disconnect here with admin rights. The fact that I am the primary account admin and can’t even make myself an owner of a board that was created by an employee so that I can change the automations is baffling. As an admin, I want the ability to see and change everything that happens in our account so that I know it’s working properly and that everything is getting done when it needs to be. As it is now, the employees that are less tech savvy or less familiar with the software are going to flounder without admin assistance which is a serious detriment to the program’s ability to manage our team’s projects and tasks.

Listen to the people, Monday!

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: Voting and Replying to keep ths alive

posts.author: SteveE (Stephen Emery)
posts.cookedHtml: This continues to be a strong desire for many instances it seems

posts.author: mark.mccoy (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an essential feature for an enterprise app. The fact that it does not currently exist is mind boggling to me. It is the main thing preventing me from recommending we roll out Monday to more departments.

posts.author: zaneta (Zaneta Grant)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone! Our product team has heard your feedback, and I’m happy to let you know this is on the roadmap for later this year. We don’t have a set ETA, but please stay tuned for more updates later on. We appreciate your input and your patience while we work on this!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you, @zaneta - We will be so happy to see this come through. Hopefully there will be a concept of a SuperAdmin vs. regular Admin? With regular Admins being what we have today.

posts.author: SteveE (Stephen Emery)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, great news thanks Zaneta!

posts.author: dfoner (David Foner)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news here ? New to Monday and this is very frustrating

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: @zoneta any update on the status of this development?

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml: This is still a GDPR issue! If we can’t administer/see boards, we can’t implement data policies.

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t think lesss than 50 votes are going to get this issue prioritized

posts.author: darrylclark (Darryl)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve inherited Monday.com ownership and we have a lot of boards and a lot of people doing things in many different ways. Because I can’t see even at the simplest level what boards are out there I don’t know who to ask what. At the very least we need to have a superuser status that would allow someone to see every board that has been created, it’s type, owner & members.

Then we need to have the permission to shut access to that board down. I know Monday.com isn’t a European company but a lot of your customers will be so GDPR is important and visibility of the estate is just the fundamental starting point to this.

Please can we have some kind of indication when greater Admin functionality is coming. This will allow us to plan accordingly.

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
@darrylclark I Agree with the points you made it is in the roadmap so Monday is aware of the issue but I would think that we would need to increase the number of votes in this topic so that @zaneta can go back to the office and show them how this issue is important and has 500 upvotes.

Since I assume that most of the people on this thread are admins and we’re outnumbered by end users by 40 to 1 ratio getting more votes is critical methinks.

posts.author: dpiunti (Derek Piunti)
posts.cookedHtml: Very frustrating that there aren’t global admin controls to allow admins to access all Workspaces/Boards. This is a deal breaker.

posts.author: LaurentConroux (Laurent Conroux)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Guys,

I agree with the Super-Admin solution, changing the Admin account behaviour now would be too dangerous in our account or in our customers accounts.

Laurent, web developer @ Synolia, platinum partner

posts.author: dpiunti (Derek Piunti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Topio:

@zoneta

@zoneta Any update on this? Very frustrating that we do not have this implemented yet.

posts.author: thejeremyg (Jeremy Gardner)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed! Between this and lack of SSO integration with O365/Azure AD for anything but the very expensive enterprise account, security on monday.com is VERY LACKING and has me ready to move my company to another solution.

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml: unfortunately admins are the minority of users and 88 votes does not get us attention from the monday.com team, I will start locking all end user accounts until they come here and start up-voting this initiative

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
For those of you who are using Enterprise, there is a new feature coming soon called Content Management. It will give admins more visibility into the structure of their account. Admins will be able to view the list of ALL boards/dashboards/docs along with their owners, subscribers, creation date, etc. as well as filter by this data.

Please note that admins will be able to view private boards/dashboards/docs in the list, however, they will NOT be able to access them!

Sneak peek screenshot below:

ENT_content_management1657×597 80.2 KB

There also are other Enterprise features under development to allow more role-based permissions as well.

Anyone who would like to learn more about whether Enterprise is right for you, our team is happy to give you a personal tour and answer all your questions (including pricing). You can book your private Enterprise strategy session here: https://meet.polishedgeek.com/more-with-monday

posts.author: BizFocusedHarry (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Admin permissions are still super restrictive.

I’ve started at this company in operations - and need visibility company-wide to be effective in all departments.

I need access to every single board, and the board members are often missing my requests to join

Needs to be a “super admin” that can see everything

posts.author: AppManIDFA (Jelle)
posts.cookedHtml: Exactly, I need this option as well!

posts.author: JoachimVeulemans (Joachim Veulemans)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a lot of boards and need this option!

posts.author: Sinister-Jay (Jordy G.)
posts.cookedHtml: When will this Feature become available?

posts.author: Topio (Javier Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear VIsitor if you’re visiting this topic because you’re interested in a solution
please be so kind to go to the very top and Vote for this feature, items that reach critical masss (200+ votes) are the ones that get addressed by the development team. We’re very, very far from that number and that is one of the reason this has been open for more than three years

posts.author: RS128 (Ray Short)
posts.cookedHtml: Just adding weight to this - it’s actually a serious governance concern.

posts.author: Nekura (Alex Kalomas)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d also like such a super-user feature, I was very surprised admins don’t already have permissions like these.

posts.author: Rhys_Cook (Rhys Cook)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Everyone,

I can see this has been running a while now but I would like to chime in here too.
We are seeing issues where users lose track of boards or want support without us having access.
Which is manageable but annoying. As the super admin I should be able to see all boards somehow no? Well that makes sense to me at least.

Kind regards,
Rhys

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

The content directory is currently available on our Enterprise plans. Please visit this article for all the information you need on the feature: Content directory

This allows admina to view and manage all workspaces, boards, dashboards and workdocs!

For those users that are not currently on an Enterprise plan, we have taken your feedback onboard, and noted your comments for review with our product team

posts.author: StefanF (Stefan Franke)
posts.cookedHtml:
I Dont think, that this is a good solution. What about all the smaller companies, which are not using enterprise plan? They have to live with the problem?

It would really be nice, if there would be a solution for all users, no matter how much they pay for a plan, when we talk about a core function.

posts.author: Larryr20 (Larry Rabinowitz)
posts.cookedHtml: If you ask for a different image dancing on your screen when you change a status, I bet you will get a response

posts.author: Philipp_Nies (Philipp Nies)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Bianca, dear Monday.com team,
Whilst having Enterprise Plan ourselves, I have to disagree that me (as an admin) have the capability to manage workspaces, boards, etc. Yes, I can SEE them, but I CANNOT manage them.
I would want to be able to have the same rights as the owners in order to ensure adequate content is being shared, only relevant people have access, etc.
I believe this is not possible via the Content directory… unfortunately…

posts.author: Udi (Udi Galezer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Philipp_Nies !

Actually you can have the ability to edit content owners and subscribers (including adding yourself ) , but you’ll need to opt into this option by contacting your monday.com representative or by opening a support ticket.

Please see this comment that was recently added to the relevant support article

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks to everyone for voting and sharing thoughtful feedback. We’ve discussed allowing admins to administer all boards with the relevant team. While they understand why this feature is important, as of now, it is not in our upcoming roadmap. Every quarter, we need to make difficult decisions about how we prioritize building new features, improving old ones, and ensuring overall platform quality.

Requests from our community, help guide the team in prioritizing features that our customers care about most so keep the votes and ideas coming because your feedback makes a difference.

Have another idea? Submit it here.

posts.author: Philipp_Nies (Philipp Nies)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Udi ,
unfortunately, this is not possible for shareable boards (and private ones). I may understand the reason to keep the private ones private, but why would I not have access to the shareable boards as an administrator? If there is a certain governance that needs to be adhered to and I identify that this is not applied, I should have the ability to make the necessary changes accordingly.
Still don’t understand why this is so difficult to implement.

posts.author: Udi (Udi Galezer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Philipp,
What I mentioned above is also true for private and shareable boards.
If you open a request you will be granted an option, as an admin, to edit content owners and subscribers from the content directory for all board types- main, private and shareable.
Regards,
Udi

posts.author: Philipp_Nies (Philipp Nies)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Udi,
That’s indeed very interesting, because our account manager stated that "Admins can access all boards and workspaces that have under the Content directory. Note, however that even if you’re an admin, you wouldn’t be able to have access to private or shareable boards that you’re not part of. ".

@AdielIsrael and @Udi, may I kindly ask you to align and to see what options we actually have for our Enterprise Plan?

Thanks.
Philipp

posts.author: RS128 (Ray Short)
posts.cookedHtml:
“This allows admina to view and manage all workspaces, boards, dashboards and workdocs!”

That’s not really true though. Admins can see all workspaces and objects in a workspace, but if they are set to private we can’t actually get into the object to view / edit content or assign owners. This is potentially a huge issue with both operational and data governanc and one we have been pushing for a while now.

posts.author: RS128 (Ray Short)
posts.cookedHtml:
What I mentioned above is also true for private and shareable boards.

No, it isn’t. I know this for a fact because I am the original admin for our enterprise plan and I cannot access any boards marked private. It is very worrying that the people on here that are supposed to be providing expert support and advice are so misinformed on basic governance like this.

posts.author: Udi (Udi Galezer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Ray!

You are correct that by default admins do not have this capability.
However, as I mentioned above, it is possible to add this option for accounts per request. This is due to legal considerations.
If you open a request you will be granted an option, as an admin, to edit content owners and subscribers from the content directory for all board types- main, private and shareable.
I would like to assure you that this is 100% accurate.

Regards,
Udi

posts.author: RS128 (Ray Short)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Udi, I will be tasking this up with our customer rep as we were told repeatedly it wasn’t possible.

R

posts.author: Lucas1 (Lucas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes this seems like a no brainer. Please Monday.com at the very least create the option to automatically set the admin as board owner when a board has no owners left, and when an owners account has been removed from the company account.

If an employee leaves a company or makes a mistake by leaving a private board the admin should be able to take over the ownership of the board, this prevents clutter and creates a failsafe to losing important data…

posts.author: Ghita (Ghita)
posts.cookedHtml: please put this on the roadmap or crate a new kind of admin that can at least see the board name and creator we can’t go on without knowing what’s is created and by Whom.

posts.author: Ghita (Ghita)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Juliette,

can you please revise the decision and put this on the Roadmap? this function is necessary for admin to at least be able to see the Board created in the workspace even if it’s without the capacity to change anything , i Know you have this option in the Entreprise Plan , but we need it as well in the Other plans.

thank you,

posts.author: Brett.Costello (Brett)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Chiming in on agreement of this being an issue.

What seems the simplest option is another higher level ‘System Admin’ level of Admin function.
We utilise a dedicated, highly controlled system admin account set up for this purpose to manage all Integrations both using Make.com (a third party add on doing highly custom data automations) and internal automations. This account allows us the best control and security practice, HOWEVER limitations in M.com require us to have this user manually subscribed to every single board we use (lucky 20-40 boards) and when we had an account issue and had to restore ALL access again it was terribly manual and caused all integrations to fail.

Very strange this is not standard feature, vote for getting it added.

Thanks,
Brett Costello

posts.author: Stefan (SJN)
posts.cookedHtml: @Brett.Costello : The true issue here is that private boards cannot be seen by any user other than the one who created them. So the suggestion of adding a (self-created) ‘system user’ will not work, unless every single person in the organisation adds this system user to their own private boards (which they wont obsiously).

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone, Thanks for sharing your feedback. I met with the responsible product team and confirmed that these capabilities are available for enterprise accounts. To clarify:

  1. Admin awareness of shareable boards is indeed supported for enterprise accounts (admins can see such boards exist, but cannot access them if not invited)

  2. Admin board control capabilities without being board owners can be done for enterprise accounts, by contacting your monday.com representative. Due to legal and privacy policies this capability is only available when requested.

There are various factors and trade-offs that need to be taken when prioritizing permissions and every couple of months, our product team reviews our pricing policy, and user capabilities.

If you feel that this does not answer your request or have further ideas, feel free to open a new request.

posts.author: DrGerry (Gerald)
posts.cookedHtml:
Admins should be “Admins” and be able to “see” all boards, even private boards. If you’re worried about an Admin seeing data in a private board, they shouldn’t be an Admin in the first place.

Yes, our account Admin has the ability to be aware of a Private board, but unless they know what the name of the private board is, they will have a very difficult time finding it. Especially when an account has multiple workspaces and boards.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Make Admins Great Again! HAHA. But its true. I’ve never encountered a platform where Admins cannot access everything. Esp. because of the stupid thing where only admins and update creators can delete an update, and only admins can delete updates created by automations.

posts.author: Stefan (SJN)
posts.cookedHtml:
It will most likely not be news to you that there’s only one reason for this, and one reason only: the revenue model of Monday. When you upgrade your plan to Enterprise (instead of Pro) you will be able to do this. This is covered with the ‘content directory’, which is only available to the Enterprise plan:

Support

Content directory

4 min read Feature   Note: The content directory feature mentioned in this article is available to the admin(s) of an account on an Enterprise plan only.     As an account admin, having th...

What is funny, is that Monday (on that page) underlines/admits the need for such a feature, in order to be able to properly administer boards:

“As an account admin, having the ability to view and manage the contents of an account at a high-level is crucial to ensure that things remain organized and secure at all times.”

By that statement, every argument thrown by the Monday helpdesk why this is not available to all administrators, on each plan, is basically irrelevant, and underlines that it only comes down to the revenue model of Monday being the true and single argument.

Also the thing that your Monday rep needs to be asked to allow for full control, this has to do with ‘privacy regulations’:

" Note: Using this feature, admins will only be able to view the name and high-level information about private Boards/Dashboards/Workdocs in the list. If you’d like to have the option to access the boards shown here, please contact your monday.com representative."

All in all: A Pro-administrator is basically not an administrator on Monday.

(!) Be aware this topic will mostly be closed soon, as it has been tagged with ‘released’, as if a solution has been released to cover the request. The ‘solution’ is the content directory, which -again- is only available to the Enterprise plan.

posts.author: davide.monticelli (Davide)
posts.cookedHtml:
Pro admin Is not an admin
Really strange

posts.author: TJP (Thilo Pukall)
posts.cookedHtml: A Super Admin account is vital, I have refused to use monday this far in my organization because the admin was too annoying with people coming and going into and out of the company. We own multiple companies and use partly monday with them, but for me the admin is really annoying. Because I refused to use it, when problems arise they are not on my desk, which is great. As the owners of the monday accounts can not set me as super admin, I have less work. Which is great now that I think about it, please do not activate super admin roles!
